articles.title: Why traditional robotics data collection is obsolete and what replaces it
articles.summary: The Robot Report podcast features Rhoda AI co-founder and chief scientist Eric Chan discussing the robotics industry's training-data bottleneck. Rhoda AI is developing a direct video action model that uses internet video data to help robots learn complex tasks more efficiently and potentially support zero-shot learning in real-world deployments.
articles.companies: ["Rhoda AI", "NVIDIA", "Google", "WorldLabs", "Yamaha Robotics Group", "maxon", "AWS", "Brain Corp", "Fictiv", "Harmonic Drive", "PickNik Robotics", "RealSense", "Robust AI", "Tesla", "Toyota Research Institute", "WTWH Media"]
articles.advisor_relevance: The article highlights a key constraint in commercial robotics: collecting enough high-quality physical-world training data. If video-based approaches can reduce data collection costs and improve robot generalization, it could accelerate adoption of physical AI across automation, logistics, healthcare, and humanoid or mobile robot applications.

articles.title: OpenAI Adds Chrome Extension to Codex, Letting Its AI Agent Access LinkedIn, Salesforce, Gmail, and Internal Tools via Signed-In Sessions
articles.summary: OpenAI has released a Chrome extension for Codex, its AI coding agent, allowing it to perform browser-based tasks directly in Google Chrome on macOS and Windows. The extension can interact with signed-in websites, use Chrome DevTools, and execute multi-step workflows across browser tabs, including services such as LinkedIn, Salesforce, Gmail, and internal tools.
articles.advisor_relevance: This highlights the shift from AI as a text or coding assistant toward AI agents that can operate across real business applications. For advisor conversations, it supports discussion of productivity gains, deeper enterprise AI adoption, and the need to monitor security and governance as agents gain access to signed-in tools and workflows.

articles.title: Will Fiber and Photonics Define Nvidia’s Next AI Data Center Push? - Data Center Knowledge
articles.summary: Data Center Knowledge highlights the question of whether fiber and photonics will play a defining role in Nvidia’s next phase of AI data center expansion. The article framing suggests continued focus on the infrastructure needed to support AI workloads, particularly high-speed connectivity within data centers.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters because AI growth is increasingly tied not only to chips but also to the supporting data center infrastructure, including networking, fiber, and photonics. For advisor conversations, it reinforces the broader AI investment theme beyond semiconductor compute into enabling technologies that may support scaling AI workloads.

articles.title: Nyobolt raises funding to bring fast charging to more robots
articles.summary: Nyobolt raised $60 million at a valuation of over $1 billion to accelerate development of fast-charging power systems for autonomous machines and AI infrastructure. The company reported fivefold year-over-year revenue growth and said demand is rising across physical AI applications, including autonomous mobile robots, industrial automation, and AI data center infrastructure.
articles.advisor_relevance: This highlights continued investment in the infrastructure layer supporting AI adoption, beyond chips and software. For advisor conversations, Nyobolt’s funding and revenue growth point to rising demand for power, charging, and energy-storage technologies that enable robotics, automation, and AI data centers to operate with less downtime.

articles.title: Wirehouse watch: Wells Fargo debuts AI-powered advisor platform - InvestmentNews
articles.summary: Wells Fargo has debuted an AI-powered advisor platform, according to InvestmentNews. The development highlights continued adoption of AI tools within wirehouse wealth management to support advisor workflows.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters because AI-enabled platforms may change how advisors manage client service, workflows, and practice efficiency. It also signals that large wealth management firms are continuing to invest in AI capabilities that could shape competitive expectations across the advisor channel.

articles.title: RingCentral adds Shopify, Calendly, and WhatsApp to AI Receptionist
articles.summary: RingCentral expanded its AI Receptionist product, AIR, with integrations for Shopify, Calendly, and WhatsApp, moving the tool beyond call answering into order inquiries, appointment scheduling, messaging, shared SMS inboxes, and call queues. The company said more than 11,800 businesses use AIR and cited customer examples showing reduced wait and hold times.
articles.companies: ["RingCentral", "Shopify", "Calendly", "WhatsApp", "Keller Interiors", "Lowe\u2019s Home Improvement", "Maple Federal Credit Union", "IDC"]

articles.title: Chris Hohn’s hedge fund slashes $8bn Microsoft stake in warning over AI disruption - Financial Times
articles.summary: Financial Times reports that Chris Hohn’s hedge fund has sharply reduced an $8bn stake in Microsoft. The move is framed as a warning about potential AI-related disruption, suggesting that even major technology incumbents may face investor scrutiny as AI reshapes competitive dynamics.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters for advisor conversations because Microsoft is widely held across growth, technology, and broad equity portfolios. The reported stake reduction highlights that AI is not only viewed as a growth driver for mega-cap technology companies, but also as a source of disruption and valuation risk that investors may need to monitor.

articles.title: AI data center bans are rapidly multiplying across the US — 69 jurisdictions block new builds, with four moves noted as permanent - Tom's Hardware
articles.summary: Tom's Hardware reports that restrictions on new AI data center construction are expanding across the U.S., with 69 jurisdictions blocking new builds. The article notes that four of these actions are described as permanent, signaling rising local resistance to AI infrastructure expansion.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters because AI growth depends heavily on physical infrastructure, including data centers, power access, and local approvals. Rising jurisdictional bans could create bottlenecks, increase project uncertainty, and affect the investment outlook for companies tied to AI infrastructure buildouts.

articles.title: Brain Corp reports strong results from AI-powered shelf-scanning robots at Czech retailer Albert
articles.summary: Brain Corp reported strong pilot results from AI-powered shelf-scanning robots deployed with Albert, a leading Czech retailer owned by Ahold Delhaize. The BrainOS-powered system reportedly exceeded a 90% accuracy baseline, performing in the high 90s for identifying products, price tags, and exceptions, while improving over time in live store conditions. Albert views the technology as part of a broader push toward digitalization, automation, and data-driven retail operations.
articles.advisor_relevance: This article highlights a practical enterprise AI use case where automation is being applied to improve retail execution, reduce manual workload, and support better inventory visibility. For advisor conversations, it reinforces that AI adoption is extending beyond software and into physical operations, with potential implications for retailers, robotics providers, and companies enabling real-world AI deployment.

articles.title: Big Tech’s $725bn AI spending spree sends free cash flow to a decade low - Financial Times
articles.summary: Financial Times reports that Big Tech’s AI-related spending has reached $725 billion, pushing free cash flow to a decade low. The article highlights the financial pressure created by the scale of AI investment, even among the largest technology companies.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters for advisor conversations because it frames AI as both a major long-term growth theme and a near-term pressure on cash flow and shareholder returns. It may help explain why investors are increasingly focused on whether large AI investments can translate into durable revenue growth and acceptable returns on capital.

articles.title: Principal Eyes $3 Billion for Two Data Center Funds on AI Boom - Bloomberg.com
articles.summary: Principal is reportedly targeting $3 billion for two data center funds, reflecting investor interest tied to the AI boom. The focus on data center fundraising highlights how AI-driven computing demand is influencing real asset and infrastructure investment opportunities.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters for advisor conversations because it shows AI-related demand extending beyond software and semiconductors into physical infrastructure such as data centers. It can help frame discussions about broader AI investment exposure, including real assets and private market strategies, while noting that fundraising targets do not guarantee investment outcomes.

articles.title: Nvidia Places Massive AI Infrastructure Bet on IREN’s 5 GW Pipeline - Data Center Knowledge

articles.advisor_relevance: This matters because AI growth increasingly depends on physical infrastructure, including data centers and large-scale power availability. For advisor conversations, the article highlights that AI opportunities may extend beyond semiconductors into companies positioned around energy, compute capacity, and data center development.

articles.title: Geopolitics Is Rewriting Memory Sourcing
articles.summary: Geopolitical tensions, especially U.S.–China policy friction, are reshaping DRAM, NAND, HBM, and advanced memory sourcing from globally optimized supply chains toward more regionalized and compliance-sensitive models. The article argues that OEMs need to prioritize resilience through qualification planning, supplier roadmap alignment, selective inventory buffers, and regional supply-chain strategies rather than relying on traditional price-cycle sourcing habits.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters because AI infrastructure depends heavily on advanced memory such as HBM and DRAM, and export controls or regional sourcing requirements can affect availability, pricing, and deployment timelines. For investors, the article highlights that semiconductor supply-chain resilience, not just demand growth, is becoming a key factor in evaluating companies exposed to AI hardware, industrial electronics, autos, telecom, and defense-adjacent markets.

articles.title: AI agent identity: how to govern agentic AI in 6 stages - VentureBeat
articles.summary: VentureBeat highlights the need to govern agentic AI through an identity-focused framework organized into six stages. The article appears to center on how organizations can manage AI agents as they become more autonomous and operationally embedded.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters because agentic AI introduces new governance, security and accountability considerations for enterprises adopting AI. Advisors can use this topic to discuss how AI adoption may create both productivity opportunities and operational risks across technology and enterprise software markets.

articles.title: Mitsubishi Heavy Industries to revamp gas turbine production process to meet growing demand from AI data center sector - Data Center Dynamics
articles.summary: Mitsubishi Heavy Industries plans to revamp its gas turbine production process in response to growing demand tied to the AI data center sector. The article indicates that AI-related data center growth is increasing demand for power infrastructure, including gas turbines.

For advisor conversations, it supports the view that AI-related capital spending may create opportunities across the broader infrastructure and energy value chain.

articles.title: Siemens Scores Win on EU Push to Streamline Industrial AI Rules - Bloomberg.com
articles.summary: Bloomberg reports that Siemens scored a win related to an EU effort to streamline rules for industrial AI. Based on the available excerpt, the development appears tied to regulatory simplification for AI use in industrial settings, though specific rule changes are not provided.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters because clearer or simplified AI rules in Europe could reduce compliance friction for industrial technology companies and support broader enterprise AI deployment. For advisor conversations, it highlights how regulation remains a key factor shaping the pace and economics of AI adoption beyond consumer and software markets.

articles.title: Inference-Time Refinement Closes the Synthetic-Real Gap in Tabular Diffusion
articles.summary: Researchers introduce TARDIS, an inference-time refinement framework for synthetic tabular data generation that improves outputs from a frozen pre-trained diffusion model without retraining the backbone. Across 15 benchmarks, TARDIS improved downstream-task performance versus real-data-trained models and outperformed the TabDiff backbone on all datasets while maintaining comparable fidelity, diversity, and sample-level privacy.
articles.advisor_relevance: This research highlights a potential shift from expensive model retraining toward more efficient inference-time optimization, which could lower costs and improve performance for AI systems using structured enterprise data. For advisor conversations, it supports the broader theme that AI productivity gains may come not only from larger models and more compute, but also from software techniques that make existing models more useful and efficient.

articles.summary: Florida Governor Ron DeSantis signed a bill adding regulations for "hyper-scale" AI data centers. The available excerpt does not provide details on the specific requirements, affected operators, or implementation timeline.
articles.advisor_relevance: This highlights the growing role of state regulation in AI infrastructure buildout. Advisors can use it to discuss how policy and permitting may influence the pace, cost, and geographic distribution of AI data center investment.

articles.title: SGS awards Dobot robotics ISO 10218 cybersecurity certification
articles.summary: SGS verified that Dobot’s CR 30H Series collaborative robots meet the cybersecurity-related safety requirements of ISO 10218-1:2025, the latest international standard for industrial robot safety. The assessment covered areas such as threat modeling, access control, secure communications, configuration protection, interface management and secure software updates, supporting Dobot’s readiness for advanced industrial applications and global market access.
articles.advisor_relevance: This highlights the growing importance of cybersecurity and safety certification as robotics becomes more connected to factory networks, control systems and enterprise platforms. For investors, it reinforces that adoption of advanced robotics may increasingly depend not only on performance and automation benefits, but also on compliance, cyber resilience and trust in industrial environments.

articles.title: Intelligent CCTV for Urban Design: AI-Based Analysis of Soft Infrastructure at Intersections
articles.summary: The study presents an AI-enabled computer vision framework that uses existing CCTV infrastructure to evaluate temporary traffic-calming measures such as pedestrian refuges and curb extensions. In Minneapolis intersections, the approach found reductions in vehicle speeds and pass-through traffic after interventions, supporting AI as a tool for rapid, low-cost transportation policy evaluation.
articles.advisor_relevance: This highlights a practical, non-consumer AI use case where existing infrastructure can be upgraded through software analytics to improve public-sector decision-making. For advisor conversations, it reinforces that AI adoption is broadening into smart cities, infrastructure management, and safety applications beyond large technology platforms.

articles.title: Samsung and new firm M3 to develop floating data centers - Data Center Dynamics
articles.summary: Data Center Dynamics reports that Samsung and a new firm, M3, plan to develop floating data centers. The provided excerpt does not include additional details on timing, scale, location, or intended customers.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters because AI growth is increasing attention on data center capacity, power availability, and new infrastructure designs. Floating data centers could represent another approach to meeting compute demand, but the limited details provided make the near-term investment implications unclear.

articles.title: How BSC Contributes to Europe’s Hybrid Quantum Strategy
articles.summary: Spain is positioning the Barcelona Supercomputing Center as a key testbed for Europe’s hybrid quantum strategy, integrating exascale supercomputing, AI, and quantum hardware through MareNostrum 5 and the MareNostrum ONA quantum partition. The effort reflects a broader European push for technological sovereignty, supported by Spain’s €808 million Quantum Technologies Strategy and EU initiatives such as the Quantum Flagship.
articles.advisor_relevance: The article highlights how quantum computing is moving from research toward strategic infrastructure, with Europe investing heavily to reduce dependence on U.S. technology leaders. For advisors, this supports conversations about long-term innovation themes tied to AI, high-performance computing, and sovereign technology investment, while also reinforcing the need for caution around near-term commercialization timelines and market hype.

articles.title: Carroll County to address data center uncertainty amid yearlong moratorium - Baltimore Sun
articles.summary: Carroll County is preparing to address uncertainty around data centers while a yearlong moratorium remains in place. The available excerpt does not provide details on the specific policy issues, affected projects, or stakeholders beyond the county-level review.

articles.title: Build a CloakBrowser Automation Workflow with Stealth Chromium, Persistent Profiles, and Browser Signal Inspection
articles.summary: The article is a tutorial on using CloakBrowser, a Python-friendly browser automation tool with Playwright-style APIs in a stealth Chromium environment. It covers setup steps, browser binary preparation, handling a common Colab asyncio loop issue, persistent profiles, and browser signal inspection.
articles.advisor_relevance: This highlights continued developer interest in automation tools that can simulate or manage browser activity, which may be relevant to discussions about AI-enabled workflows, data collection, and productivity tooling. articles.title: University Claims Withholding Water From Nuclear Weapons Data Center Is 'Unlawfully Discriminatory' to Data Centers - 404 Media
articles.summary: A university is reportedly claiming that withholding water from a nuclear weapons data center is unlawfully discriminatory toward data centers. The dispute highlights growing tensions around data center access to critical local resources such as water.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters because data centers are increasingly central to digital and AI infrastructure, but their growth can face local resource, permitting, and legal challenges.
